title: Please walk me through parking permits
filename: Please walk me through parking permits _ r_ucla.pdf

segments.author: Specialist_Cloud7507
segments.text:
Please walk me through parking permits

Hello, I am a commuter, I need to park my car to go to class, Im completely lost as to what im supposed to do. I go on bruin e permit and there are so many options and for the summer? I want a permit for the fall quarter, please help! thank uuu

segments.author: anishinaabish
segments.text: Do you need to be on campus every day? I was able to just buy $7 daily passes coming to campus for 3 days a week.

segments.author: Specialist_Cloud7507OP
segments.text: i’m also on campus 3 days a week do u recommend the daily pass or the one with the application?

segments.author: anishinaabish
segments.text: I can't remember what the quarterly cost was exactly, but I think it was $350+. For me it made sense and it might for you too because $7x3 days a week= $21 per week x 10 weeks = ~$210 per quarter, which was for sure cheaper than the permit price. Even if you had to go a few extra days for finals or extra curriculars like I did you'd be good. This is the daily parking permit website I used: Bruin ePermit - Bruin ePermit Portal

segments.author: Specialist_Cloud7507OP
segments.text: do you have to purchase the daily pass everyday? also thank you ur replies are very helpful

segments.author: anishinaabish
segments.text: Yes, purchase every day that you come to campus. I would set a reminder with my wake up alarm so I would remember to do it as soon as I woke up. If you wait too late in the day all of the on-campus lots will sell out so you'd have to park in Lot 32 in Westwood and walk farther. Also, no problem I wish I had someone to tell me this when I was trying to figure it out last year!!!

segments.author: glizzyglobber6699
segments.text: is the 7$ all day parking or is it just 1-3 hours. also which parking lot is 7$? I can only find 17$ whole day

segments.author: Slow-Back-5949
segments.text: You have to go to add/ edit parking application and just rank the structures!

segments.author: Specialist_Cloud7507OP
segments.text: and that’s it? that’s the application?

segments.author: Slow-Back-5949
segments.text: I called and they said thats it! Ive also never done it before but they sent an email confirmation and said you’ll get an update September 2nd I think. If you are filing for special permits Im not sure if you have to do it through that link instead but as a commuter I don’t think thats necessary.

segments.author: Specialist_Cloud7507OP
segments.text: whats a special permit? also were there only 4 parking choices for you too?

segments.author: Local-Tear9723
segments.text: omg the parking situation at ucla is so confusing so i totally get it. basically you have to apply for a permit for the quarter through the bruin epermit portal online. it's kinda like a lottery system and not everyone gets one. you'll see a window of time when the application is open and you have to do it then. when you apply you rank all the parking structures in order of your preference, and my advice is to rank all of them to increase your chances. if you only come to campus a couple days a week, it might be cheaper to just buy the daily pass instead of getting a full quarter permit. i know a few 1 people who gave ur/pu colan the Soeffaicrciahl liont rs/ uacnlda just rent a private garage from a locaCl roevaetre by ucla on prked.
